title: Stelis sclerophylla
taxonomy: Stelis sclerophylla is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Stelis, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Stelis is further divided into several subgenera, and Stelis sclerophylla falls under one of these subgroups. The Orchidaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as orchids. Stelis sclerophylla is native to Central and South America, where it can be found growing in tropical and subtropical regions. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the orchid family.

intro: Stelis sclerophylla, also known as Mexican Stelis, is a species of orchid native to tropical regions of Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Stelis sclerophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
